calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: J. SCOTT MCCAY, ESQ., 925.932.6095, FOR PETITIONER MANSA CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION DBA GREEN BANKER.
ARTHUR LIPTON, ESQ., 415.362.6286, FOR RESPONDENT WILFRED SHAW.

*MINI MINUTES PART 1 OF 2*

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: RESPONDENT WILFRED SHAW'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is denied. Petitioner Mansa Construction Corporation filed a petition to confirm arbitration award. Petitioner also filed and served a civil case cover sheet, petition to confirm arbitration award, final award, notice of hearing on petition to confirm arbitration award, memorandum of points and authorities, and declaration of J. Scott McKay on Respondent Shaw. All of these documents together will be referred to collectively as the "Petition and supporting papers." CCP 1290.4(b) required that the Petition and supporting papers be served "in the manner provided by law for the service of summons in an action." (CCP 1290.4(b)(1)).

The Petition and supporting papers were served by substitute service on Shaw on September 4, 2009. CCP 415.20(a). This included mailing a copy of the Petition and supporting papers. Id.

"Service of a summons in this manner is deemed complete on the 10th day after the mailing." CCP 415.20(a), which was September 14, 2009. From September 14, 2009, Shaw was then entitled to 10 days to respond to the Petition. CCP 1290.2. That section provides that "[a] petition under this title shall be heard in a summary way in the manner and upon the notice provided by law for the making and hearing of motions, except that not less than 10 days' notice of the date set for the hearing on the petition shall be given." CCP 1290.2.

Shaw contends correctly that the hearing on the Petition should not have been noticed at least until September 24, 2009, when he had at least 10 days notice, counting from the date service of the Petition and supporting papers was effective. (Shaw also contends that another 5 days for service by mail under CCP 1013(b) should have been added, such that the hearing could not be held until September 29, 2009. As set forth below, I do not need to reach that issue.)

The hearing was noticed for and the Petition to confirm arbitration award was heard on September 21, 2009. The arbitration award was confirmed by Order of the Court on September 21, 2009. Judgment confirming the arbitration award was dated that day and filed on September 22, 2009. *SEE PART 2 OF MINI MINUTES*

Respondent Wilfred Shaw Motion For Set Aside/Vacate Judgment (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Feb-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: J. SCOTT MCCAY, ESQ., 925.932.6095, FOR PETITIONER MANSA CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION DBA GREEN BANKER.
ARTHUR LIPTON, ESQ., 415.362.6286, FOR RESPONDENT WILFRED SHAW.

*MINI MINUTES PART 2 OF 2*

Nonetheless, Shaw's motion to vacate judgment entered after the hearing on September 12, 2009 is denied because the judgment is not void. The Court had jurisdiction over Shaw on September 4, 2009 when substituted service of the Petition and supporting papers was complete. (CCP 1290.4(b).) Respondent concedes there was personal jurisdiction over him as of September 14, 2009. It is undisputed that the Court has subject matter jurisdiction over petitions to confirm arbitration awards. There is no evidence that Shaw did not have actual notice of the hearing. At most, the Court was in excess of its jurisdiction under CCP 1290.2 by confirming the award on September 21, 2009, on less than 10 days notice of the date set for hearing. See People v. American Contractors Indemnity (2004) 33 Cal. 4th 653, 660-661.

Respondent did not directly challenge this judgment under CCP 473 or CCP 473.5. (See American Contractors, supra, 33 Cal. 4th at 663 n.8.) In fact, this motion to vacate was filed more than three years after judgment was entered. Shaw has not shown any lack of knowledge about the judgment. To the contrary, in his moving papers, he admits that since the judgment, "significant enforcement ensued, consisting of an order of examination, charging order, issuance of writs of execution, and other like processes." Respondent's MPA at 3, l. 4-5. Nor has Respondent shown any unusual circumstances that prevented him from seeking this relief by motion earlier. See American Contractors Indemnity, supra, 33 Cal. 4th at 661. The motion to vacate is untimely and it is denied.

The Court does not address Respondent's other points about the whether the name on the arbitration award would preclude enforcement of the arbitration award, since this was not raised in his notice or moving papers as a grounds for this motion, and it was raised by him only in the context of the further steps that could be taken if the "relief sought to vacate the judgment" was granted. (Resp. MPA at 6, l.22-23. See id. at 2, l. 14-16.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
